figures.caption: Figure 1: Reverse-time trajectories over each model’s path space. Masked diffusion is discrete but gradient-free; continuous diffusion is gradient-based but requires a terminal projection; SJD couples ambient gradients with sticky jumps onto anchors.

Figure 3: Text8 valid-word counts at length thresholds ≥ 5 and ≥ 6, as a function of the blendingkernel bandwidth σW , across NFE budgets. CANDI corresponds to σW = 0 (W = I); MDLM is the degenerate-kernel limit.

Table 4: Frozen frequency-keyed hazards on Text8 (plain cross-entropy (14), W = I , η = 1): valid-word fraction at length ≥ 5 under the offline protocol of Section E.2, mean±std over three seeds. Anti-frequency schedules commit common characters first in reverse time; frequency-keyed schedules commit them last. No non-uniform schedule clears uniform under the pre-registered mean−std criterion at either budget.

Figure 4: Effect of η on the state-dependent hazard target λ̂t(y, a) under a single anchor a = 1 for reverse trajectories (circles mark commitment events). As η → 1, the width of the intensity surface widens. As η decreases from 1.00 (d) to 0.25 (a), the variance of the unsticking kernel sharpens relative to the mixture marginal pact . High intensity regions contract toward the anchor mean, and trajectories in low intensity regions tend to later commitments.

Figure 5: Quality–diversity frontiers on Text8, by NFE budget. Each curve is a temperature sweep in the (unique-word fraction, valid-word fraction at length ≥ 5) plane; upper-right dominates. The SJD family at σW ≥ 1.0 Pareto-dominates CANDI and MDLM at high diversity.
